Title: Tiong calls for urgent talks over diesel price impact on tourism
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has instructed his ministry to initiate immediate consultations with the Finance Ministry over the impact of rising diesel prices on the tourism transport sector.
In a statement, Tiong said his ministry is closely and carefully monitoring the recent increase in diesel prices and understands the challenges faced by operators, particularly the sudden rise in operating costs for those heavily dependent on diesel.
"If this matter is not handled properly, it could have a ripple effect on inbound tourism, businesses, and the overall stability of the tourism ecosystem.
"I have received feedback from associations and relevant stakeholders and have instructed the ministry to immediately initiate consultations with the Finance Ministry.
"These discussions will focus on the current situation and consider targeted measures, including temporary, time-bound support to help ease short-term pressures arising from the current situation, partly influenced by developments in the Middle East," he said.
Recognising that the tourism transport sector is a key pillar of the national tourism industry, Tiong said while addressing current pressures, the government must also consider the long-term health of this sector.
"Beyond cost issues, it is equally important to continue strengthening safety standards, vehicle performance, and the quality of services provided to visitors.
"In this regard, any measures taken will be guided by an approach to stabilise and upgrade.
"Our goal is to help operators navigate this challenging period while encouraging gradual improvements within the sector, including vehicle renewal, appropriate financial support, and overall enhancements to the regulatory framework.
"I would like to emphasise that the government's objective is not merely to mitigate the immediate impact, but to build a more resilient and competitive tourism transport system.
"Any form of support introduced will be designed with the industry's long-term development in mind, rather than as temporary fixes or one-off solutions," he said.
Tiong urged industry players to remain engaged in constructive dialogue and cooperate to stabilise the sector ahead of Visit Malaysia Year 2026.
He called on all parties to remain calm, act prudently, and not be driven purely by emotion.
"The government will do its utmost to balance the various interests involved and seek reasonable, compromise-based solutions.
"While the measures introduced may not fully satisfy all parties, we will continue striving to implement the most appropriate arrangements, taking into account overall feasibility and the long-term interests of the industry," he said.
As of March 18, the price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has risen to 80 sen to RM4.71 per litre, while in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, it remained at RM2.15 per litre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran's new Supreme Leader absent on Persian New Year as speech read out on TV
Article:
TEHRAN: People in Iran eagerly awaited a first public appearance by the country's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, on Persian New Year, or Nowruz, on Friday - but a speech attributed to him was instead read out on state television.
On the screens, a still image of the supreme leader was shown alongside an Iranian flag, reported German news agency dpa.
Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared in public or released audio or video messages since his appointment on March 8, leading to speculation about his health.
He succeeded his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an Israeli airstrike on Tehran on the first day of the US-Israeli attack on Feb 28.
Mojtaba Khamenei also lost his wife, mother and a brother-in-law in the bombings.
According to the US government, Mojtaba Khamenei is injured. No further details are available.
Nowruz, meaning "new day," is an ancestral festivity marking the first day of spring and the renewal of nature. It marks the spring equinox in the northern hemisphere, when night and day are of equal length, and falls on March 20 or 21.
In his Nowruz statement, Khamenei thanked the president, the government, the armed forces and the Iranian people for their resistance against the joint US-Israeli attacks.
"The adversaries were not relying solely on military means such as missiles, drones and torpedoes", he said, adding that Iran's "front line" was broader than its enemies wished to acknowledge.
Khamenei declared the new Persian year the "year of the resistance economy in the spirit of national unity and security."
The supreme leader's message also urged all media outlets to exercise restraint and avoid highlighting vulnerabilities, warning that doing otherwise could help the enemy achieve its objectives.
He also denied that Iranian forces had carried out attacks on Turkey and Oman.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Towards a more caring, united and resilient society
Article:
I would like to extend my warmest wishes of Selamat Hari Raya Aidilfitri, Maaf Zahir and Batin to all Muslims in Malaysia.
This year's Aidilfitri is observed in a spirit of reflection, as the world continues to grapple with ongoing conflict in the Middle East, alongside many global challenges that are affecting lives everywhere.
Here at home, Malaysians are also facing rising living costs and economic uncertainties that demand resilience, prudence, and unity.
In these challenging times, I wish to call upon our Muslim friends to continue embracing the true values of Aidilfitri — moderation, gratitude, compassion, and care for those in need.
Let us celebrate with simplicity and avoid excess, in line with the spirit of Ramadan which teaches sacrifice, discipline, and empathy.
At the same time, let us strengthen the bonds of unity and solidarity, extending a helping hand to one another regardless of background.
As many families navigate financial pressures, it is important to practise prudent spending and responsible financial management for the wellbeing of both households and the wider community.
May this Aidilfitri serve as a renewed beginning for all of us to reaffirm our shared values, strengthen our humanity, and work together towards building a more caring, united, and resilient society.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
National Unity Advocate
Kuala Lumpur
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King hosts Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, the King and Queen of Malaysia, today graced the Hari Raya Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara here.
The event was attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, other cabinet ministers and representatives of foreign diplomatic missions, including those from the United Kingdom, Iran, Azerbaijan and Indonesia.
Among the more than 600 guests were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Malaysian National News Agency chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai and editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj.
Among the dishes served were Johor specialities such as kacang pool and sup tulang merah, as well as lemang, ketupat, rendang and nasi beriani.
The event was enlivened by Hari Raya songs performed by the police Quintet Band, as well as a special performance by singer and Yayasan Kebajikan Artis Tanahair chairman Datuk DJ Dave.
Their Majesties also took time to mingle with the guests.
Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Raja Zarith Sofiah attended Aidilfitri prayers at the Istana Negara Main Surau.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US allows 30-day sale of Iran oil at sea in bid to tame prices
Article:
WASHINGTON: The Trump administration has issued a 30-day sanctions waiver allowing the sale of Iranian oil currently at sea, a strategic move aimed at cooling global energy prices amid the ongoing conflict with Tehran.
The waiver, announced on Friday by the US Treasury Department, permits the purchase of approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian oil.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the move is designed to relieve pressure on energy supplies and "use Iranian barrels against Tehran" to keep prices down during the military campaign, dubbed Operation Epic Fury.
Global oil prices have surged by about 50% since the US and Israel launched strikes on Feb 28.
The White House is increasingly concerned that high energy costs will hurt businesses and consumers ahead of the November midterm elections.
Under the licence, which remains in effect until April 19, Iranian oil may be imported into the US if necessary to complete a sale or delivery, though it remains unclear if any will reach American soil.
The move is primarily expected to benefit China, the top buyer of Iranian crude.
Running out of options
The waiver is the third time in two weeks the Treasury has eased sanctions on adversaries to tame a market that has seen prices soar above US$100 a barrel.
Previous waivers were issued for Russian oil stranded at sea, including a specific 30-day licence on March 5 for India to purchase Russian supplies.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said these supplies could reach Asian markets within three to four days and be refined over the next six weeks.
However, analysts remain sceptical of the long-term impact. Brett Erickson of Obsidian Risk Advisors said price control efforts may be limited until the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.
The strait, a conduit for 20% of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas, was effectively closed by Iran following the outbreak of hostilities.
"The easing of sanctions raises concerns about the rapid depletion of Washington's economic toolkit.
If we've reached the point of loosening sanctions on the country we are at war with, we're really running out of options," Erickson said.
Domestic measures
In a further attempt to stabilise costs, the administration on Wednesday announced a 60-day waiver of the Jones Act shipping law.
This temporarily allows foreign-flagged vessels to move fuel, fertiliser, and other goods between US ports.
Despite the temporary easing, the administration maintains it is keeping "maximum pressure" on Iran's ability to access the international financial system.
Mark Dubowitz, CEO of the Foundation for the Defence of Democracies, said on X that the decision was a "smart move" to help win the fight against the regime.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aidilfitri tragedy as fire guts three terrace houses in Kota Baru
Article:
KOTA BARU: Hari Raya Aidilfitri turned tragic for three families today when their double-storey terrace houses were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Bunut Payong here this morning.
The 8.27am blaze left two units completely gutted, while a third was 80 per cent destroyed.
A Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 8.27am.
Fire engines from the Kota Baru and Tunjung stations, supported by a water tanker from Pengkalan Chepa, were dispatched to the scene.
"Upon arrival, the operations team found three double-storey terrace houses on fire.
"Two units were 100 per cent destroyed, while another was 80 per cent burnt. No casualties were reported," the spokesperson said, adding that firefighting operations are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: One colour, one heart: 52 family members coordinate for Raya
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: For the past eight years, the family of Muda Abdullah and Khatijah Said has turned heads every Hari Raya with their tradition of wearing matching festive attire.
Muda, 68, and Khatijah, 62, view the coordinated outfits as more than just a style choice; it is a symbol of the togetherness they have built within their large family.
Since 2018, the family has rotated through various themes, including emerald green, navy blue, purple, sage green, soft grey, maroon, royal blue and this year's choice, brick orange.
The selection process is democratic, with the family of 52 — comprising 12 children, 10 in-laws and 30 grandchildren — voting on three proposed colours to ensure everyone is satisfied with the final decision.
"When we vote, everyone is happy. No one feels left out. While the colour is the same, they can choose their own patterns, though we prefer plain designs to keep it looking neat and harmonious," Muda said at his home in Kampung Tok Dir today.
Muda admitted that coordinating such a large group is no small feat, but said the sight of his children and grandchildren in unison makes the effort worthwhile.
"When I see everyone in the same colour, it feels like we are of one heart. It is wonderful to see the compatibility and harmony among the children, in-laws and grandchildren," he said.
Despite the significant expenses involved in preparing outfits and providing duit raya for such a large brood, Muda does not view it as a burden.
"It costs a bit of money, but that is fine. The satisfaction comes from seeing the family happy. On Raya morning, seeing everyone laughing and smiling makes any problem disappear," he added.
Beyond the fashion tradition, the family has maintained another staple for over 20 years: a signature lamb gulai, cooked together every year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haiyaa: Uncle Roger to 'slim down' operations after announcing outlet closure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Restaurant chain "Fuiyoh! It's Uncle Roger" says it will be "slimming down" its operations after announcing the closure of its branch at the IPC Shopping Centre.
The company said that when it started one-and-a-half years ago, it grew too quickly and signed leases in locations that were not the right fit in a rush to capitalise on early momentum.
"Instead of expansion, we are now slimming down our operations and running a lean team, something we realised we should've done from the start."
The chain, known for its fried rice, also said it was reworking the menu to include more protein and sides to ensure customers feel they are getting better value for money.
"All full-time employees have received severance, and we wish them the best with what comes next," it said, adding that its outlets in KL, Selangor, Johor and KLIA Terminal 2 remain operational.
In 2024, Malaysian comedian Nigel Ng, better known as "Uncle Roger" announced he would open his
first restaurant
in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran fires missiles at joint US-UK base in Indian Ocean
Article:
WASHINGTON: Iran recently fired two ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia, suggesting Tehran possesses a longer strike range than previously estimated.
Iran fired two ballistic missiles toward the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, according to a report by The Wall Street Journal on Friday citing US officials.
Neither missile hit the target, which is about 4,000km from Iranian territory. However, the report said the launch suggests Tehran has missiles with longer ranges than previously thought.
One missile failed in flight, while the other was targeted by an interceptor fired from a US warship, though it was unclear if the missile was hit. The Pentagon did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Diego Garcia, located in the Chagos Islands, is one of two bases Britain allows the United States to use for operations regarding Iran. American forces station bombers and other equipment at the base, which serves as a key hub for operations in Asia, including previous bombing campaigns in Afghanistan and Iraq.
Britain has agreed to hand the Chagos Islands back to Mauritius after holding the archipelago since the 1960s. It maintains a lease for the base on Diego Garcia, the largest of the islands.
US President Donald Trump has criticised the decision.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kampung Batu Enam skies light up in 40-year Raya eve tradition
Article:
KUALA NERUS: For more than 40 years, the skies over Kampung Batu Enam have been illuminated by a spectacular fireworks display to mark the eve of Hari Raya Aidilfitri.
The annual tradition sees crowds and residents gather at the Batu Enam roundabout to witness a display that typically lasts for over an hour.
Resident Ismail Harun, 40, said the scale of the activity depends on the supply of fireworks pooled by the community.
"The fireworks display in this village has become a major attraction on the eve of Aidilfitri.
"Usually, the roads leading to the village roundabout will be congested with visitors eager to see the various types of fireworks set off by residents," he said.
Ismail added that the activity is a casual affair that serves as a reunion for those returning to their hometown for the festivities.
Visitor Muhammad Ismadi Alias, 38, said the village's reputation for its fireworks tradition is well-known.
"Various types of fireworks are set off here, drawing large crowds. It is only on the eve of Aidilfitri that I get to see so many fireworks being played at once," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NST Leader : Making balik kampung safer
Article:
THE great Malaysian balik kampung ritual to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri is also an uncomfortable paradox.
It's a fun but stressful time to journey back home in all parts of the nation but it's also a precarious trip fraught with traffic mishaps, some deadly.
While the odds of getting into a traffic altercation is dire at 2,000 accidents in a single day, the data states that 1 in 81,300 travellers per trip will be killed, based on 2025 festive season data.
This is what happens on the period: the North-South Expressway traffic is traversed by 2.3 million vehicles daily.
Traffic police statistics compiled from their Ops Selamat operations between 2022 and 2025 reaffirms: about 31,000 accidents, 461 of them fatal, causing 495 deaths, including 211 motorcyclists.
The only positive aspect? Total accidents dropped from 12,407 to 9,859 in 2025.
Deaths dropped from 140 to 77, thanks to vigilance and safety awareness by authorities and motorists alike.
The first day of Aidilfitri is always the deadliest: in 2022, after pandemic restrictions were lifted, 28 deaths were recorded, 19 of them motorcyclists.
The leading causes were negligence, loss of control, entering the opposite lane and fatigue.
Accidents have been conscientiously mitigated: infrastructure has vastly improved: known black spots widened, brighter markings on stretches rife with head-on collisions, better lighting at rest and recreations entries and exits, where drowsy-driver accidents cluster and better signage on last-mile rural roads.
Thousands of police officers supervise roadblocks, rest areas and high-risk stretches to enforce speed limits and monitor fatigue.
PLUS' smart highways that utilises dynamic digital signboard messaging to adjust speed limits in real time based on traffic density and weather has reduced collisions during peak congestion.
Since fatigue and drowsiness caused 20 fatal crashes in 2023, PLUS expanded the R & R pitstops to include dedicated sleeping zones.
The Malaysian Institute of Road Safety Research (MIROS) has a sound advice: drivers should stop every two hours regardless of alert levels.
Travellers should avoid departing on the day before Raya eve and Raya eve itself but leave three to four days earlier.
Work commitments render this strategy impractical but data shows that accidents spike highly on these prescribed Raya period.
Perhaps the authorities could give tax breaks or more subsidies to train and bus operators to entice motorists to become commuters, just for the Raya season.
The 2025 Op Selamat drop of 41 per cent in fatal crashes, despite more accidents, is the outcome of better enforcement, more free rail/bus alternatives and effective public communication on departure timing.
The balik kampung ritual is revered but lives of loved ones are always at stake despite safer driving and riding initiatives.
It's up to the motorists to take heed to avoid becoming the next tragic statistic.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iconic Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Raya family photos, says Hannah Yeoh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has invited Muslims celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri today to visit the newly revitalised Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad for family photos.
In a Facebook post wishing Malaysians "Selamat Hari Raya", Yeoh highlighted the building's recent upgrades.
"If you have time, stop by Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad in Kuala Lumpur with your family. The photos would be pretty as the building has been upgraded," she said.
Block One of the Sultan Abdul Samad Building Complex opened on Feb 2, marking the first completed phase of Khazanah Nasional Bhd's Dana Warisan restoration initiative.
Restoration works focus on safeguarding the building's character as a national heritage site while enhancing safety, accessibility and the overall visitor experience.
Visitors can now enjoy a range of offerings at
Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad
, including immersive exhibition spaces and galleries, a one-stop visitor centre, experiential retail and craft-making spaces, as well as lettable event halls and venues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah-based journalists find new meaning in Raya away from home
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri away from family for the first time has proven to be a meaningful experience for Kuala Lumpur native Syed Iylia Hariz Al Qadri Syedn Izman, who is marking the occasion in Sabah this year.
The 37-year-old Bernama journalist moved to the state in October last year and decided to remain for the festivities to experience the local atmosphere.
"This year I decided to celebrate Raya here because I wanted to experience the Hari Raya atmosphere in Sabah.
"My in-laws are also coming to celebrate with me here, so it gives a more special meaning even though I am far from my hometown," he said.
Syed Iylia, whose grandparents are in Kuala Lumpur and his wife is from Perak, said he misses the tradition of gathering on the first day of Raya.
"Of course, I miss seeking forgiveness in the morning and enjoying traditional dishes together. I try to recreate that by connecting online with family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating my home," he said, describing Sabah's celebrations as relaxed and culturally diverse.
He said he tried to create the festive atmosphere by connecting online with his family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating his home.
On the differences in celebration, he described Sabah's Raya celebrations as more relaxed and culturally diverse.
For Penang-born Zacherlyn @ Noor Zafira, a reporter with a local daily who has been in Sabah for 16 years, the high cost of festive air travel is a major hurdle to returning home.
However, her situation is also marked by loss.
[[nid:1401306]]
"I no longer have anyone as all my family members have passed away since 2022, and that was the last year I returned home for Hari Raya," she said.
Despite this, she said what she misses most are the moments spent with the loved ones who raised her.
"What I miss are the moments with my late grandfather, grandmother and aunts; the food they prepared, the happiness at home and the laughter we shared.
"These memories remain close to my heart as strength while I continue building my own life," she said.
To maintain the festive mood, Zacherlyn keeps things simple by buying Raya cookies, cleaning her home while listening to Raya songs, decorating with ketupat replicas and watching festive shows.
She also attends open houses when invited and occasionally revisits old family photos to relive past celebrations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ten killed, four missing in South Korea car plant fire
Article:
DAEJEON: Ten people were killed and four remain missing after a massive fire broke out at a car parts plant in Daejeon on Friday.
The blaze, which started at about 1.17pm, left 59 others injured. Authorities said nine of the bodies were recovered from the third floor of the factory, while one was found on the second floor.
A total of 170 workers were inside the building when the fire began. Search operations were delayed until 10.50pm on Friday as experts assessed the structural integrity of the plant and managed 200kg of sodium, which posed an explosion risk.
Firefighters are currently using rescue dogs and tearing down internal structures to locate the four missing people.
"We plan to deploy rescue workers and continue the search for those missing," an official said.
-BERNAMA-YONHAP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Guan Eng, Chow in fresh spat over Penang quit rent hikes
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The open exchanges between Bagan member of parliament Lim Guan Eng and Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow over Penang's quit rent hikes show no signs of abating.
Lim has intensified his criticism, claiming that many voters would not have supported the state leadership if they had known the 2023 state election manifesto allowed for such steep increases in quit rent rates.
He expressed shock at Chow's recent remarks that an election manifesto is not a binding document and does not restrict the state from introducing new policies.
Lim said this includes significant quit tax hikes deemed necessary for economic stability.
Lim stressed that the "Kita Penang, Kita Boleh" unity manifesto launched ahead of the last state polls did not indicate that the quit rent hikes could be increased by thousands of times.
"There was no indication to us as candidates then that the manifesto allowed such drastic hikes.
"Many voters would not have supported the chief minister if they had known this," he said in his social media posting.
In his recent press conference, Chow addressed criticism that the state government's initiatives were not part of its election manifesto, saying such arguments were misplaced.
"The manifesto outlines what a party commits to when contesting elections, and we report progress on those commitments annually in the state assembly," he said.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/03/1400321/chow-rebuts-claims-being-out-touch-community
Chow cited the establishment of the Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC) as an example, noting that it was not included in the 15th state election manifesto, but was still implemented due to current economic needs.
Lim urged the state government to honour its manifesto pledge to ensure that no group is marginalised and that development benefits all segments of society.
Describing the increases as excessive, he cited several cases involving sharp hikes affecting landowners across the state, including in Batu Kawan.
He said some landowners experienced increases running into hundreds or even thousands of times, causing distress and financial strain.
Lim also claimed his office had received numerous complaints, including from a landowner in Simpang Empat, who is struggling with declining business and loan commitments following a sharp rise in the quit rent hike.
He questioned Chow's assertion that he had not received many complaints during his engagements statewide, asking whether the chief minister had sufficiently met affected constituents in Batu Kawan.
Lim further criticised the state government for defending the accuracy of the quit rent calculations, noting that several cases saw reductions of between 80 and 90 per cent after being raised.
"If the system is correct, why are there such significant reductions? This shows the calculation system is flawed and needs to be reviewed," he said.
Lim has maintained a steady stream of criticism against Chow since the new quit rent rates took effect at the beginning of the year.
He called for a reassessment of the quit rent rates, urging the state government to consult stakeholders and introduce a fairer and more reasonable mechanism.
Lim added that the government should prioritise the people in line with its principles.
Chow recently dismissed claims that he was out of touch with grassroots concerns, stressing that his frequent statewide engagements, including the Jelajah Ramadan programme, demonstrated otherwise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: One killed, five injured after car veers off road while avoiding dog
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: A 43-year-old foreigner died, and five others were injured when their car veered off the road while swerving to avoid a dog at Jalan Garing, Machap Baru, yesterday.
The deceased, a man, died while receiving treatment at Alor Gajah Hospital following the 4.15pm accident.
Melaka Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Superintendent Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the Perodua Myvi was carrying six people, including five Indonesians and a four-year-old Malaysian child.
Preliminary investigations found that the driver tried to avoid a dog crossing the road, causing the car to skid to the left shoulder and plunge into a ditch.
"The 39-year-old driver sustained chest and leg injuries, while a 40-year-old foreign woman suffered injuries to her right hand and left leg. The child sustained minor injuries," he said in a statement.
Two other passengers, aged 28 and 52, suffered facial, rib, and body injuries.
"All victims were rushed to Alor Gajah Hospital for treatment," Zaki said.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Those with information are urged to contact the investigating officer, Inspector Loh Gia Xao, at 019-5382353 or visit their nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King, Queen perform Aidilfitri prayers at Istana Negara [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia, performed Aidilfitri prayers at Surau Utama, Istana Negara today.
In attendance were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, alongside Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and his wife, Datin Seri Hamidah Khamis.
Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.
The congregation included Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh and several foreign representatives.
Their Majesties and the congregation recited the Aidilfitri takbir before performing the prayers, led by Istana Negara religious officer Datuk Munir Md Salleh.
In the sermon titled 'Syawal: Bangkit Dari Ujian, Bersatu Dalam Keimanan', Munir reminded the congregation not to forget those in the Middle East currently facing severe conflict and unrest.
He called on Muslims to strengthen unity, avoid division and preserve peace within society.
The people were also urged to practice prudence and avoid excessive spending in view of the uncertain global situation caused by international conflicts.
Following the sermon, Their Majesties spent time greeting congregants before departing for an Aidilfitri gathering.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US sending Marines and amphibious assault ship to Middle East, officials say
Article:
WASHINGTON: The US military is deploying 2,500 Marines and an amphibious assault ship to the Middle East, officials said on Friday, as President Donald Trump accused NATO allies of "cowardice" for not assisting in reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
The narrow waterway, which carries roughly 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas, has been closed to most traffic since the US and Israel launched a war against Iran nearly three weeks ago.
Vital energy infrastructure across the region has been targeted, causing oil prices to jump about 50% since the conflict began on Feb 28. To mitigate a global economic shock, the Trump administration said it would waive sanctions to allow the sale of 140 million barrels of Iranian oil currently stranded on tankers.
The war has claimed more than 2,000 lives, primarily in Iran and Lebanon. A recent Reuters/Ipsos poll indicates that two-thirds of Americans believe Trump will order a large-scale ground invasion, though only 7% support such action.
On Friday, the Israeli military carried out two waves of air strikes on Tehran and central Iran, targeting missile production and storage sites. Iran responded with missile attacks on Tel Aviv and Jerusalem, where fragments landed near the Old City.
Troop Deployment
Three US officials said 2,500 Marines, the USS Boxer, and accompanying warships are deploying to the region. While their specific roles remain unclear, officials said no decision has been made on whether to send troops into Iran itself.
Trump said the US is close to its goals of degrading Iran's military and preventing nuclear weapon development, suggesting the military effort may soon wind down.
However, he criticised allies for refusing to help secure the Strait of Hormuz. While some have pledged support, France and Germany said fighting must stop first. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is expected to speak with Trump this weekend.
Leadership Uncertainty
Questions have arisen regarding the condition of Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, who has not been seen in public since his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed on the war's first day.
While a message of defiance was issued in his name during Nowruz, a US intelligence official said his continued absence raises questions about who is currently running the country.
Meanwhile, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they attacked Haifa and Tel Aviv with multi-warhead missiles and targeted US bases in Bahrain with drones. Iranian state media also reported that intelligence minister Esmail Ahmadi was killed in a suspected Israeli assassination.
Fuel prices climb ahead of U.S elections
Rising fuel prices are threatening Trump's political standing ahead of the November congressional elections. Brent crude remains near US$110 a barrel following massive disruptions to global supplies.
Global oil flows have dropped by 12 million barrels per day—about 12% of global demand—due to export halts and output cuts. On Friday, Iraq declared force majeure on all oilfields developed by foreign companies, further straining the market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thousands gather at Masjid Al Bukhary for Aidilfitri prayers
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The spirit of Aidilfitri filled the air in Penang as nearly 5,000 Muslims gathered at Masjid Al Bukhary here this morning to perform the Aidilfitri prayers in a vibrant and meaningful celebration.
Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib and his wife, Toh Puan Raja Noora Ashikin Raja Abdullah, joined the congregation in observing the two-rak'ah sunat prayer, marking the end of Ramadan.
The prayers and sermon, titled 'Aidilfitri Yang Dinanti, Redha Ilahi Yang Dicari', were led by the Grand Imam of the State Mosque, Mohammad Zaki Ibrahim, as worshippers began arriving as early as 7.30am, filling the mosque grounds with a festive atmosphere.
This year's prayers were held at Masjid Al Bukhary for the first time as the Penang State Mosque is currently undergoing renovation.
Earlier, Ramli arrived at the mosque about 8.30am and was received by Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid, state Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari and senior government officials.
Following the prayers, many Muslims took the opportunity to visit the graves of their loved ones before continuing Aidilfitri traditions with visits to relatives and friends.
Across the state, Aidilfitri was celebrated in a spirit of unity and togetherness, reflecting Penang's multicultural harmony.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, in his Aidilfitri message, said the occasion was a meaningful moment to strengthen bonds, foster goodwill and nurture unity among the people.
"Aidilfitri is a meaningful moment to strengthen ukhuwah, foster closer ties and sow the values of love and unity within society.
"These values form the foundation of harmony among Penangites who live in mutual respect despite differences in religion, culture and background," he said.
Chow said the state government remains committed to strengthening Islamic affairs as part of its inclusive development agenda, noting that progress is not solely measured by physical and economic growth but also by the strengthening of religious institutions, spiritual education and community wellbeing.
He said RM25.34 million has been allocated this year for the development of mosques and surau statewide, an increase from RM17.67 million in 2025, with 258 mosques currently in operation, including seven newly completed last year.
In education, he said, Penang now has 352 Islamic schools and 222 registered Quran and Fardu Ain classes, with continued financial assistance channelled to religious schools and teachers.
Touching on the uniqueness of this year, Chow noted that the Chinese New Year coincided with Ramadan, with celebrations observed in moderation out of respect for the holy month — reflecting the strong spirit of mutual understanding among communities.
He also called on the people to uphold unity and act as "agents of peace" by avoiding provocations that could disrupt harmony, especially as the state prepares to welcome visitors for Visit Malaysia 2026.
"Let us enliven Aidilfitri with visits, forgiveness and togetherness in the spirit of unity, while showcasing Penang as a harmonious state to the world," he added.
Chow also reminded the public to prioritise safety when travelling during the festive period, expressing hope that Aidilfitri would be celebrated in joy and harmony with loved ones.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
Article:
SAN FRANCISCO: A federal jury in California found on Friday that tech tycoon Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders, causing a share price plunge as he prepared to buy the company for US$44 billion.
The verdict in the class action securities lawsuit means the world's richest person could be ordered to pay billions of dollars in damages.
Minutes after the judgment, Musk's lawyers said they would appeal the decision, describing it as a "setback".
After a three-week trial involving in-person testimony from Musk, the jury found that two tweets posted in May 2022 contained false statements responsible for the share price drop.
Investor Giuseppe Pampena filed the suit on behalf of those who sold Twitter shares between mid-May and early October 2022.
Musk acquired the platform in late October 2022 and later renamed it X.
Jurors agreed Musk violated a securities rule barring false and misleading statements that sink a stock price.
Musk did not immediately react to the verdict.
The judgment is a rare legal defeat for Musk, who is often called "Teflon Elon" for his ability to emerge from lawsuits unscathed.
His lawyers said a Texas court cleared him the same day in a separate defamation case.
In 2023, a jury in the same San Francisco federal court cleared him of similar charges brought by Tesla shareholders following his 2018 tweets about taking the automaker private.
The California civil complaint accused Musk of driving down Twitter's stock price to gain leverage to renegotiate the purchase price or exit the deal, causing shareholders to lose money.
At one point, Musk tweeted the deal was on hold until executives proved the percentage of "bots" — fake accounts — was as low as claimed.
The plaintiffs said these statements were designed to pressure the board into accepting a lower price while Tesla's share price was falling, as Musk would have needed to sell more Tesla shares to finance the acquisition.
Musk abandoned his effort to exit the deal in late 2022 after the company took him to court to uphold the contract.
Forbes magazine earlier this month estimated Musk's net worth at US$839 billion, based primarily on his stakes in Tesla and SpaceX.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd
